created_components.runner_support: scripts/train_controlled_v6b_minimal.py optional Stage45-C support recovery flags

findings.note: single-class NOT_ENTITLED holdout; not informative for SUPPORT/REFUTE robustness

stage45b1_diagnosis_summary.diagnosis: Internal-only family holdouts show asymmetric entitlement robustness: REFUTE is robust across every entitled holdout family, while SUPPORT is brittle, especially under the paraphrase and primary_failure_type=none holdouts. Many gold-entitled (mostly SUPPORT) examples are over-rejected as NOT_ENTITLED. This is an internal-only analogue of the NOT_ENTITLED collapse seen in external diagnostics, produced entirely from internal controlled data.

target_failure_mode.secondary: entitled-to-NOT_ENTITLED over-rejection (both SUPPORT and REFUTE, but most acute for SUPPORT)
target_failure_mode.not_targeted: REFUTE recall (already robust in every internal family holdout observed)

allowed_inputs: internal training split model output logits
Stage45-B1 recovered family fields (intervention_type, primary_failure_type, and their composites)
internal Stage45-B family holdout dev split for reporting/selection only (never for the auxiliary loss)
disallowed_inputs: any external examples, labels, metrics, or prediction distributions
dev/holdout labels inside the auxiliary training loss
default_behavior_when_disabled: All Stage45-C flags default off/0.0; when disabled or all weights are 0.0, no auxiliary loss term is added to total_loss and existing training behavior is preserved exactly. Report fields are still emitted with stage45c_enabled=false.

support_recovery_term.formula: mean(relu(1 - softmax(logits)[:, target_label_id])) over gold target_label training rows
support_recovery_term.inputs: output['logits'] and train_inputs['final_labels'] from the internal training split only
support_recovery_term.inactive_value: zero-valued, gradient-connected tensor (logits.sum() * 0.0) when no gold target_label rows are present in the batch

entitled_ne_over_rejection_term.formula: mean(softmax(logits)[:, NOT_ENTITLED_id]) over gold entitled (--stage45c-entitled-labels, default SUPPORT,REFUTE) training rows
entitled_ne_over_rejection_term.inactive_value: zero-valued, gradient-connected tensor when no gold entitled rows are present in the batch

auxiliary_loss_design.integration: total_loss = total_loss + support_recovery_weight * support_recovery_loss + entitled_ne_penalty_weight * entitled_ne_penalty_loss, added after the existing label/frame/predicate/sufficiency/polarity/intervention losses and before other optional auxiliary BCE terms

expected_evaluation_protocol: Enable Stage45-C alongside Stage45-B1 family holdout (--stage45-use-family-holdout --stage45-family-field intervention_type --stage45-holdout-family paraphrase, or --stage45-family-field primary_failure_type --stage45-holdout-family none) to test whether the auxiliary terms improve SUPPORT recall on the held-out internal family.
Compare stage45b_holdout_metrics.support_recall and not_entitled_prediction_rate with Stage45-C enabled vs. disabled, holding all other flags fixed.
Use only the internal holdout dev split for this comparison; do not introduce Stage43-B1 or other external data into the comparison.
Existing Stage44-B2 prior-aware selection may still be used for checkpoint selection, restricted to the internal holdout dev split.

runner_family_holdout_compatibility.behavior: Stage45-C reads only train_inputs['final_labels'] and output['logits'] from whichever train split is active (family-holdout train split when Stage45-B1 is enabled, or the normal internal train split otherwise). It does not alter how the family holdout split is constructed.

allowed_claims: Stage45-C introduces an internal-only SUPPORT entitlement recovery scaffold.
Stage45-C targets internal SUPPORT under-recall and entitled-to-NOT_ENTITLED over-rejection.
Stage45-C does not use external data.
Stage45-C remains compatible with Stage45-B1 family holdout diagnostics.
disallowed_claims: Do not claim external validation PASS.
Do not claim VitaminC transfer success.
Do not claim naturalistic fact-verification generalization.
Do not claim Stage45-C solves external collapse before a new held-out external evaluation.
Do not use Stage43-B1 for tuning or final validation.
remaining_risks: The auxiliary terms are diagnostic/regularization signals derived from training-split labels the model already sees via the main label loss; measured internal-holdout gains may be modest or may trade off against REFUTE/NOT_ENTITLED precision.
Internal family holdout robustness remains an internal proxy and is not equivalent to external (VitaminC/Climate-FEVER/naturalistic) generalization.

If --stage45c-entitled-labels or --stage45c-target-label is misconfigured to a label absent from the internal training split, the corresponding term silently stays inactive (zero-valued) rather than failing; this is intentional per the no-crash/no-NaN requirement but should be checked in stage45c_loss_terms_active and stage45c_train_*_count before interpreting results.
Combining Stage45-C with --stage45-use-family-holdout on a family whose train split has zero SUPPORT or REFUTE rows will leave the corresponding term permanently inactive for that run.
next_stage: Stage45-D: internal-only ablation comparing Stage45-C on/off across multiple Stage45-B1 holdout families, still restricted to internal controlled data, before any new external evaluation is proposed
